org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism

Interface Node<G extends Graph<?,N,A,E>,N extends Node<G,?,A,E>,A extends Anchor<G,N,?,E>,E extends Edge<G,N,A,?>>

  • Type Parameters:
    G - is the type of the graph.
    N - is the type of the node inside the graph.
    A - is the type of the anchors inside the graph.
    E - is the type of the edges inside the graph.
    All Superinterfaces:
    Comparable<ModelObject>, ModelObject, Serializable
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    AbstractFSMNode, AbstractNode, AbstractStandardNode, FSMEndPoint, FSMStartPoint, FSMState, StandardMonoAnchorNode, StandardMultiAnchorNode

    public interface Node<G extends Graph<?,N,A,E>,N extends Node<G,?,A,E>,A extends Anchor<G,N,?,E>,E extends Edge<G,N,A,?>>
extends ModelObject
    This is the semantic object for nodes.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:02:58
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND, Marc BAUMGARTNER
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-formalism

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addAnchor(A anchor)
      Attached a anchor to this Node.
      void addAnchor(AnchorLocation location, A anchor)
      Attached a anchor to this Node.
      void disconnect()
      Disconnect the node from the edges.
      List<A> getAnchors()
      Get the anchor list of this Node.
      List<E> getEdges()
      Reply all Edges attached to this Node by the way of Ports.
      String getExternalLabel()
      Replies a string that may be used to put on the size of the edge.
      G getGraph()
      Get the Graph of this Node
      Iterable<E> getIncomingEdges()
      Reply the incoming edges of this node.
      Iterable<E> getOutgoingEdges()
      Reply the outgoing edges of this node.
      boolean hasAnchor()
      Reply true if an Anchor is attached to this Node.
      boolean hasIncomingEdges()
      Reply if this node has at least one incoming edge.
      boolean hasOutgoingEdges()
      Reply if this node has at least one outgoing edge.
      boolean isAnchor(A anchor)
      Reply true if the given Anchor is attached to this Node.
      void removeAllAnchors()
      Remove all anchors attached to this Node.
      void removeAnchor(A anchor)
      Remove a anchor attached to this Node.
      void setGraph(G model)
      Set the Graph of this Node

      • Methods inherited from interface org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.ModelObject

        addModelObjectListener, findModelObject, fireComponentAdded, fireComponentRemoved, fireContainerChanged, fireContentChanged, fireLinkChanged, firePropertyChanged, getMetamodelSpecification, getName, getProperties, getUIEditableProperties, getUUID, getViewBinding, removeModelObjectListener, setName, setProperties, setUUID

      • Methods inherited from interface java.lang.Comparable

        compareTo

    • Method Detail

      • getExternalLabel

        String getExternalLabel()
        Replies a string that may be used to put on the size of the edge. Basically it is composed of the name of the edge replied by ModelObject.getName() and any other label added by the subclasses.
        Returns:
        the external label that is describing this node.

      • getGraph

        G getGraph()
        Get the Graph of this Node
        Returns:
        a Graph.

      • setGraph

        void setGraph(G model)
        Set the Graph of this Node
        Parameters:
        model - a Graph.

      • getAnchors

        List<A> getAnchors()
        Get the anchor list of this Node.
        Returns:
        a list of Anchor.

      • addAnchor

        void addAnchor(A anchor)
        Attached a anchor to this Node.
        Parameters:
        anchor - the new Anchor.

      • addAnchor

        void addAnchor(AnchorLocation location,
             A anchor)
        Attached a anchor to this Node.
        Parameters:
        anchor - the new Anchor.
        location - the location of the new Anchor.
        Since:
        0.4

      • removeAnchor

        void removeAnchor(A anchor)
        Remove a anchor attached to this Node.
        Parameters:
        anchor - the Anchor to remove.

      • removeAllAnchors

        void removeAllAnchors()
        Remove all anchors attached to this Node.
        Since:
        0.3

      • isAnchor

        boolean isAnchor(A anchor)
        Reply true if the given Anchor is attached to this Node.
        Parameters:
        anchor - the Anchor.
        Returns:
        true if anchor was attached to this Node, false otherwise.

      • hasAnchor

        boolean hasAnchor()
        Reply true if an Anchor is attached to this Node.
        Returns:
        true if an anchor was attached to this Node, false otherwise.
        Since:
        16.0

      • getEdges

        List<E> getEdges()
        Reply all Edges attached to this Node by the way of Ports.
        Returns:
        an array of Edges.

      • disconnect

        void disconnect()
        Disconnect the node from the edges.

      • hasIncomingEdges

        boolean hasIncomingEdges()
        Reply if this node has at least one incoming edge.

        The definition of an incoming edge depends on the implementation of the node.

        Returns:
        true if the node is linked to an incoming edge; otherwise false.
        Since:
        16.0

      • hasOutgoingEdges

        boolean hasOutgoingEdges()
        Reply if this node has at least one outgoing edge.

        The definition of an outgoing edge depends on the implementation of the node.

        Returns:
        true if the node is linked to an outgoing edge; otherwise false.
        Since:
        16.0

      • getIncomingEdges

        Iterable<E> getIncomingEdges()
        Reply the incoming edges of this node.

        The definition of an incoming edge depends on the implementation of the node. For example, in not-directed graphs, this function should replies the same edges as getEdges().

        Returns:
        the incoming edges.
        Since:
        16.0

      • getOutgoingEdges

        Iterable<E> getOutgoingEdges()
        Reply the outgoing edges of this node.

        The definition of an outgoing edge depends on the implementation of the node. For example, in not-directed graphs, this function should replies the same edges as getEdges().

        Returns:
        the incoming edges.
        Since:
        16.0
